claim_boundary: Local feasibility and evidence audit only. This is not DRC evidence, ERC evidence, production routing signoff, fabrication release, or routed-board promotion approval.

existing_report_assessment.assessment: Existing production/reports/drc.json and erc.json are local candidate/fail-closed artifacts, not raw KiCad CLI DRC/ERC results. They include local CAD connection coverage and candidate metadata, not a KiCad violation report payload.

commands_run.command: command -v kicad-cli || true; command -v kicad || true; command -v pcbnew || true; command -v eeschema || true; command -v kikit || true; command -v freerouting || true; command -v java || true
commands_run.summary: The repo wrapper resolves kicad-cli 7.0.11, but its sch/pcb command families expose only export subcommands here; KiCad GUI binaries, KiKit, and freerouting were not found on PATH.

commands_run.command: scripts/kicad_run.sh kicad-cli version; scripts/kicad_run.sh kicad-cli sch --help; scripts/kicad_run.sh kicad-cli pcb --help
commands_run.summary: Local wrapper reports kicad-cli 7.0.11. Help output for sch and pcb only lists export, so required release commands sch erc and pcb drc are unavailable. A temp STEP export attempt also fails because the routed board was created by a newer KiCad file format.
Blocked: KiCad 7.0.11 cannot open the routed board because it was created with a newer KiCad file format. No release STEP output was generated.

Found one KiCad project, five PCB files including e1-phone-mainboard-routed.kicad_pcb, root and hierarchical schematics, and local STEP candidate artifacts. No DSN/SES autorouter exchange files were found.

The routed candidate board and real-footprint development board have identical SHA-256 158e862d290ccdac7086130861ca414ea50cfbc00f07c5c46b648d2330a2f59b; the schematic SHA-256 is e00ee14ca4e49ff754b788ebf534bf141a525b9ae2ed72df2716b7d10c345df5.

commands_run.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('packages/research/chip/board/kicad/e1-phone/pcb/e1-phone-mainboard-routed.kicad_pcb')
s=p.read_text(errors='ignore')
for token in ['(net ', '(footprint ', '(segment ', '(via ', '(zone ', '(gr_line ', '(dimension ')]:
    print(token.strip(), s.count(token))
PY
commands_run.summary: Raw routed candidate counts: 1371 net records, 89 footprints, 306 segments, 24 vias, 13 zones, 0 gr_lines, 0 dimensions.

commands_run.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
for f in ['packages/research/chip/board/kicad/e1-phone/production/reports/drc.json','packages/research/chip/board/kicad/e1-phone/production/reports/erc.json']:
    p=Path(f)
    d=json.loads(p.read_text())
    print(f)
    for k in ['artifact_id','status','claim_boundary','release_credit','drc_status','erc_status','tool','generated_by']:
        if k in d: print(' ',k,':',d[k])
    print(' top_keys:', sorted(d.keys())[:30])
    print(' bytes:', p.stat().st_size)
PY
commands_run.summary: Both existing report files identify as candidate artifacts and share local non-release metadata. No KiCad CLI generator/tool field or raw KiCad violation schema was found.

commands_not_run.reason: repo wrapper resolves kicad-cli 7.0.11, but this CLI lacks the required pcb drc command.
repo wrapper resolves kicad-cli 7.0.11, but this CLI lacks the required sch erc command.

repo wrapper resolves kicad-cli 7.0.11, but a temp export attempt failed because the routed board requires a newer KiCad file format; existing STEP is a local candidate artifact and must not be overwritten without a real release export run.

promotion_blockers: The available repo-wrapper kicad-cli is 7.0.11 and lacks sch erc / pcb drc, so real release DRC/ERC cannot be generated here.
The available repo-wrapper kicad-cli is also too old to open the routed board for STEP export; a temp export attempt failed with a newer-file-format error.
The routed candidate board is byte-identical to the real-footprint development board, so it has not been promoted as an independent production routed source.
The root KiCad project declares a non-release schematic scaffold claim boundary.
The root schematic explicitly says display_camera, radios, audio_buttons, and split_interconnect remain text scaffolds wired in a later pass.
Existing production/reports/drc.json and erc.json are candidate metadata artifacts, not KiCad CLI DRC/ERC outputs.
Development pad/pin coverage still has 13 pending supplier pad-map or order records.
Selected supplier response packs are absent for release-critical display, camera, USB-C/side-key, cellular, and Wi-Fi/Bluetooth domains.
Production routing still lacks supplier-approved land patterns, exact pin ordering, SI/PI/RF validation baselines, Gerber/drill/IPC output generation, and routed-board clearance release.
next_production_routing_steps: Install KiCad 9.x or the repo-approved release KiCad version with kicad-cli available in PATH.
Open or batch-load e1-phone.kicad_pro and confirm the schematic hierarchy resolves all sheet paths and symbols before ERC.
Replace remaining text scaffold sheets and supplier-pending footprints/pad orders with approved schematic symbols, footprints, pin maps, and STEP models.
Generate a fresh production board source from the approved schematic netlist, not by reusing the byte-identical development board as release evidence.
Run kicad-cli sch erc with JSON output and archive raw ERC plus reviewed waivers.
Refill zones in a controlled KiCad run, save the board revision, then run kicad-cli pcb drc with JSON output and archive raw DRC plus reviewed waivers.
Export production Gerber X2, NC drill/slots, position files, BOM, IPC-2581 or ODB++, and STEP from the same checked board hash.
Run SI/PI/RF/length-skew checks for MIPI D-PHY, PCIe, UFS, USB2, RF feeds, return paths, and PDN.
Rerun enclosure clearance against the routed STEP with supplier component models and archive first-article/fabricator release intake artifacts.
